name: Northern Ireland Honest Broker Service
description: The Honest Broker Service (HBS) is the primary route for accessing de-identified health and social care data from Northern Ireland's Health and Social Care (HSC) system for research purposes. It provides data through the Northern Ireland Trusted Research Environment (NITRE), acting as an independent intermediary between data controllers and researchers.
linkages: HSC system data across all NI trusts

requirements.name: HBS application
requirements.description: Submit a formal application to the Honest Broker Service detailing research requirements and data justification

requirements.name: Governance committee approval
requirements.description: Application reviewed by the HBS governance committee for public benefit, data minimisation, and legal basis

requirements.name: NITRE access
requirements.description: Approved researchers access data within the Northern Ireland Trusted Research Environment

access_steps.name: Initial enquiry and feasibility
access_steps.description: Contact HBS to discuss research requirements and data availability within the NI HSC system

access_steps.name: Submit HBS application
access_steps.description: Complete the formal application including study protocol, data specification, and legal basis

access_steps.name: Governance review
access_steps.description: HBS governance committee reviews the application for compliance and public benefit

access_steps.name: NITRE provisioning
access_steps.description: Approved data prepared and workspace provisioned within NITRE

timeline.notes: HBS engagement, feasibility, and application preparation
Data preparation and NITRE workspace setup

costs.notes: No fees for approved research. The Honest Broker Service is publicly funded within NI HSC.

strengths: Only route to Northern Ireland health and social care data for research
Covers the entire NI HSC system including all trusts
Honest broker model provides independent de-identification
Integrated health and social care data (NI has a combined HSC system)
limitations: Less developed infrastructure compared to other UK nations
Limited linkage options compared to England, Scotland, or Wales
Fewer researchers and projects means less community support
recent_changes: NITRE (Northern Ireland Trusted Research Environment) established and operational
Growing data holdings and linkage capabilities
Alignment with UK-wide TRE standards and HDR UK network
